claim: debt-runner is a 7-mode interactive orchestrator that sequences existing Node.js scripts with convergence-loop verification at every stage
debt-runner never writes to MASTER_DEBT.jsonl directly — all mutations go through staging files first, then through existing scripts
The warm-up before debt-runner menu shows current S0-S3 counts, effort estimates per mode, resume status, and pending staging file count
MASTER_DEBT.jsonl contains 8,470 items as of 2026-03-26: 7,281 open, 1,115 resolved, 74 false positives, 13% resolution rate
S0 has 11 open alerts (non-resolved, non-FP items); S1 has 1,259 open alerts; verification queue (NEW status) has 2,125 items

resolve-bulk.js automatically calls generate-views.js and reconcile-roadmap.js --write after every bulk resolution, and scans .claude/plans/*.md for references to resolved DEBT IDs — a side effect not documented in debt-runner SKILL.md
evidence: scripts/debt/resolve-bulk.js source code post-write side effects

claim: consolidate-all.js marks extract-sonarcloud.js as DEPRECATED — the live API sync path via sync-sonarcloud.js is canonical
debt-runner has no mode for processing raw/review-needed.jsonl — uncertain dedup candidates created by dedup-multi-pass.js have no dashboard exposure
debt-runner has no trend visualization — metrics-log.jsonl accumulates 112 entries but nothing renders time-series data
debt-runner sync mode does not use --resolve or --full flags on sync-sonarcloud.js, meaning SonarCloud items that disappeared are never auto-resolved
assign-roadmap-refs.js directly overwrites MASTER_DEBT.jsonl via writeMasterDebtSync and is manual-only with no hook, CI, or skill trigger
evidence: scripts/debt/assign-roadmap-refs.js source, no CI/hook reference found

claim: check-phase-status.js uses ES module syntax (import) while all other debt scripts use CommonJS require — a module-style inconsistency
generate-views.js --ingest triggers a full MASTER_DEBT.jsonl overwrite from deduped.jsonl — any direct edits to MASTER_DEBT made after the last dedup run but before --ingest will be silently lost
generate-views.js produces 5 views + INDEX.md from MASTER_DEBT.jsonl; it is called in CI (ci.yml line 245) for views freshness check, by consolidate-all.js, and by multiple skills

escalate-deferred.js reads from data/ecosystem-v2/deferred-items.jsonl (not the main docs/technical-debt/ tree) and calls intake-pr-deferred.js when defer_count >= 2 — the only automated promotion path from ecosystem deferral to TDMS
intake-audit.js is the canonical audit-to-TDMS funnel script — it runs multi-pass dedup, roadmap assignment, and view regeneration post-write, and is called by 13 single-session audit skills
evidence: scripts/debt/intake-audit.js source; all single-session audit SKILL.md files

claim: resolve-bulk.js is called by CI workflow resolve-debt.yml on PR merge when Resolves: DEBT-XXXX lines appear in the PR body — the only CI workflow that writes to TDMS
sync-roadmap-refs.js uses ES module syntax (import from node:fs) while all other debt scripts use CommonJS — a second ESM inconsistency
CI integration exists for only two scripts: validate-schema.js (pre-commit hook + ci.yml) and sync-roadmap-refs.js --check-only (ci.yml). resolve-bulk.js is called by resolve-debt.yml on PR merge.

All 13 single-session audit skills (audit-code, -security, -performance, -refactoring, -engineering-productivity, -ai-optimization, -documentation, -process, -enhancements, -aggregator, -comprehensive, multi-ai-audit, audit-agent-quality) are INTEGRATED with TDMS via intake-audit.js
All 8 ecosystem audits (hook, session, tdms, pr, skill, doc, script, health) use a different TDMS pathway: per-finding /add-debt for deferred items only, not batch JSONL intake
comprehensive-ecosystem-audit has NO TDMS integration — it produces a unified report but does not call intake-audit.js or /add-debt anywhere

Ecosystem audits hardcode category: 'engineering-productivity' for ALL deferred items regardless of finding domain — hook latency, JSONL sync issues, and dead skills all land in the same category, distorting TDMS category distributions
audit-process is the only skill that runs TDMS intake per stage during execution (not just post-audit), making it resilient to context compaction losses
create-audit enforces TDMS pipeline (validate-schema.js, intake-audit.js, generate-views.js, generate-metrics.js) as a mandatory gate in Phase 5 validation — new audits are born TDMS-integrated
/pr-review is INTEGRATED with TDMS with mandatory enforcement: Step 7 gates require every deferred item to have a DEBT-XXXX ID before summary; Step 5 calls /add-debt for all deferred items
/code-reviewer is DISCONNECTED from TDMS — produces code review violations but has no /add-debt reference or JSONL output path in its SKILL.md
/pr-retro actively discourages TDMS routing: REFERENCE.md states 'DEBT is NOT an option unless the user explicitly requests it. Do not offer defer to DEBT as a choice.'

session-end runs consolidate-all.js (step 7d) on every session end — this is maintenance/normalization, NOT new finding intake. New findings in Claude context that were not written to JSONL or /add-debt before session-end are silently lost
No hook script directly calls any intake script or writes to MASTER_DEBT.jsonl — all hook findings route to hook-warnings.json via append-hook-warning.js
sonarcloud.yml CI workflow runs SonarCloud analysis on every push/PR but does NOT call sync-sonarcloud.js — the bridge between CI analysis and TDMS is entirely manual
pattern-compliance-audit.yml creates GitHub Issues with label tech-debt when violations exceed thresholds — not TDMS items. This creates a parallel debt tracking channel invisible to TDMS.
known-debt-baseline.json at .claude/state/ tracks 29 cognitive-complexity and 16 cyclomatic-complexity file exceedances with no DEBT-XXXX IDs — a shadow debt store completely invisible to TDMS

escalate-deferred.js creates debt items programmatically from data/ecosystem-v2/deferred-items.jsonl when defer_count >= 2 — the only automated promotion path from ecosystem deferral to TDMS, but first deferral is invisible
pending-refinements escalation in run-alerts.js: items surfaced 3+ times auto-escalate to 'S1 DEBT candidate' alerts prompting /add-debt — an undocumented intake path from /alerts into TDMS
SonarCloud has 2,561 items via sync script and 286 via manual dashboard paste in MASTER_DEBT — confirming both pathways have been used historically
SonarCloud pagination fetches up to 10,000 issues (20 pages x 500); beyond that truncates with warning — a scale limitation in sync-sonarcloud.js
scripts/mcp/sonarcloud-server.js provides MCP tools for AI-driven SonarCloud status checks (mcp__sonarcloud__get_quality_gate, mcp__sonarcloud__get_issues) without shell invocation
Semgrep and CodeQL findings are uploaded as SARIF to GitHub Security tab only — no local artifact, no bridge to TDMS, completely invisible to the local reporting system
npm audit runs in security.js health checker for aggregate counts only — no per-vulnerability TDMS tracking, no npm audit in CI as blocking check
The Go statusline binary (tools/statusline/) has 22 widgets and zero debt-related widgets — no S0 count, no total open items, no resolution rate
unplaced-items.md is permanently stale — last written 2026-02-01, claims 0 unplaced items, is NOT regenerated by generate-views.js
metrics.json alerts.s0_count (11) counts only open (non-resolved, non-FP) S0 items while by-severity.md shows all 26 S0 items regardless of status — intentional distinction but causes user confusion
debt-health.js Technical Debt category scores 21/100 (F-grade) with all 8 metrics at poor or average — but the benchmarks are wildly miscalibrated (good=10 open items when project has 7,281)

metrics-log.jsonl has 112 entries (one per session-end run) sufficient for trend computation but schema only records total/open/resolved/s0_alerts/s1_alerts — no per-category, per-source, or age-distribution trend

BUG: debt-health.js filters avg_age with lowercase 'resolved' and 'closed' strings but MASTER_DEBT.jsonl uses uppercase 'RESOLVED' and 'FALSE_POSITIVE' — the filter never matches, causing avg_age to include all 8,470 items including resolved ones
evidence: scripts/health/checkers/debt-health.js avg_age filter; MASTER_DEBT.jsonl status values

claim: resolve-item.js calls sync-deduped.js --apply after every single-item resolution but resolve-bulk.js (used by CI) does NOT call sync-deduped.js — every CI-triggered resolution leaves deduped.jsonl out of sync with MASTER_DEBT
evidence: scripts/debt/resolve-item.js execFileSync calls; scripts/debt/resolve-bulk.js source (no sync-deduped call)

claim: BUG: dedup-multi-pass.js has no --dry-run or --force flags despite REFERENCE.md documenting them as valid invocations — the script always runs all 6 passes and writes all output files unconditionally
evidence: scripts/debt/dedup-multi-pass.js source (no arg parsing); .claude/skills/debt-runner/REFERENCE.md Dedup mode documentation

claim: BUG: TRIAGED is in resolve-bulk.js ELIGIBLE_STATUSES but not in audit-schema.json validStatuses — items with TRIAGED status would pass CI resolution eligibility but fail schema validation
VERIFIED → IN_PROGRESS transition has no script implementation — IN_PROGRESS is a valid schema status but no script ever sets it; items can only get this status via direct JSONL edits
evidence: scripts/config/audit-schema.json validStatuses; scripts/debt/*.js survey found no IN_PROGRESS setter

claim: verify-resolutions.js NEW → VERIFIED promotion only checks file existence and line count — does NOT verify the actual issue still exists; refactored files still pass promotion check
reverify-resolved.js found 32 false alarms out of 62 audited 'possibly unresolved' items (52% false positive rate) — suggesting TRUE_POSITIVE rate of keyword-proximity heuristic is only ~48%
resolve-debt.yml CI workflow does not call sync-deduped.js or reconcile-roadmap.js after resolution — post-CI state leaves deduped.jsonl diverged and ROADMAP.md stale
Resolution log (resolution-log.jsonl) has only 14 entries despite 1,115 resolved items — most resolutions bypass the log entirely
/code-reviewer is the largest single coverage gap — triggered on every code change, produces 5-30 findings per PR, zero path to TDMS
SonarCloud CI auto-sync is a trivial fix: add one CI step to sonarcloud.yml calling sync-sonarcloud.js --force --severity BLOCKER,CRITICAL,MAJOR — script is fully implemented
Ecosystem audit 'Fix Now' findings create no TDMS audit trail — fixed items are silently resolved with no MASTER_DEBT record that the issue existed or was fixed
known-debt-baseline.json has 45+ file entries (29 CC + 16 cyclomatic) ranging up to CC=189 that have no DEBT-XXXX IDs and are completely invisible to the dashboard
pre-commit-fixer Path B (defer to known-debt-baseline.json) is BROKEN as a TDMS path — the skill conflates two fundamentally different defer destinations without explaining the distinction to users
Of 18 defer-to-TDMS locations catalogued: 6 WORKING, 5 ASPIRATIONAL (suggest /add-debt but don't enforce), 1 BROKEN (routes to shadow store), 2 MISSING (no TDMS path specified)

alerts defer is ASPIRATIONAL ('suggest /add-debt') while ecosystem audit defer is mandatory ('MUST via /add-debt') — same user action word 'defer' has radically different enforcement levels across skills
Maximum interactive depth across all 13 surveyed skills is 3 levels — Level 3 is always a single-item review, never a sub-menu
evidence: SQ9 Section 3; system-test, ecosystem-health, skill-audit all confirmed 3-level maximum

claim: debt-runner already implements the best-practice flat menu + stats header + mode return pattern — expansion should be additive, not a rewrite
evidence: SQ9 Serendipity S2; debt-runner SKILL.md menu rendering

claim: The _shared/ecosystem-audit library (extracted 2026-03-25 from 8 skills) represents the most current standardized interactive pattern — debt dashboard should anchor to it for per-item triage flows
S0/S1 items must never be auto-accepted without user review — evidence from debt-runner, pr-review, and _shared ecosystem audit all enforce this same rule independently
evidence: debt-runner SKILL.md delegation protocol; pr-review DAS 3-4 mandatory user decision; _shared scope explosion guards

claim: Every interactive skill persists state to disk after every decision — no skill relies on conversation context alone for state management
evidence: SQ9 Section 7 state file table; all 9 skills confirmed state file persistence

claim: The 'you decide on S2/S3, show me S0/S1' delegation pattern is the most powerful single-statement delegation across all surveyed skills
evidence: SQ9 Section 5 delegation spectrum; alerts, debt-runner, pr-review all confirm this boundary

claim: No roadmap_ref or milestone view is generated by generate-views.js despite these fields being preserved in all MASTER_DEBT items — a missing consumer for roadmap-aligned debt visibility
evidence: scripts/debt/generate-views.js PRESERVED_FIELDS list; docs/technical-debt/views/ directory listing

claim: FALSE_POSITIVES.jsonl has only 6 entries and is not validated by validate-schema.js by default — FP items could accumulate schema violations undetected
evidence: docs/technical-debt/FALSE_POSITIVES.jsonl (6 entries); scripts/debt/validate-schema.js default target is MASTER_DEBT.jsonl

claim: sync-deduped.js failure is silently swallowed by resolve-item.js (try/catch with empty catch) — if deduped.jsonl drifts from MASTER_DEBT there is no user notification
